task_description: You are a security engineer testing code in sandboxed containers. Your goal is to create an E2B sandbox and configure it with two specific environment variables: `SECRET_TOKEN` with the value `super-secret-123` and `API_ENDPOINT` with the value `https://api.example.com`. Once the sandbox is running, execute a command to write the values of these environment variables to a file named `/home/user/env_dump.txt` in the format `TOKEN=<value>\nENDPOINT=<value>`. Finally, you must save the sandbox ID to `/home/user/e2b_task_info.json` on the host machine so the verification script can connect to it and verify the file contents. Do not kill the sandbox after you are done.
truth:
The sandbox must still be running. The file `/home/user/env_dump.txt` inside the sandbox must contain exactly:
TOKEN=super-secret-123
ENDPOINT=https://api.example.com

The host file `/home/user/e2b_task_info.json` must contain a JSON object with the key `sandbox_id`.
